takes.event_basis: <cite index="33-20">Wall Street expects revenue of $78.8 billion and earnings per share (EPS) of $1.77 for Q1 fiscal 2027</cite>; <cite index="33-4,33-5">big four hyperscalers -- Alphabet, Amazon, Meta Platforms, and Microsoft -- are forecasting combined 2026 capital expenditures of more than $700 billion, with Meta, Alphabet, and Microsoft raising their full-year capex guidance</cite>.
takes.thesis: <cite index="33-1,33-2">On Polymarket, the implied probability that Nvidia will beat expectations sits around 90%</cite>. <cite index="35-1">Based on Nvidia's track record of beating earnings expectations and the strong quarterly reports that we've already seen from the hyperscalers that are its top customers, we fully expect Nvidia to report another blowout quarter</cite>. <cite index="40-1,40-3,40-4">Goldman Sachs expects Nvidia to report a 'beat-and-raise' quarter, with its analyst expecting Nvidia's updated 2026 and 2027 estimates to be 14% and 34% above broader Wall Street expectations, respectively</cite>.
takes.risks: <cite index="35-2,35-3">The company beat consensus estimates and provided guidance that was more upbeat than the consensus last quarter, only to see its stock fall the next day; with a nearly $5.4 trillion market cap, Nvidia is so big that it really has to exceed investor expectations by a wide margin on earnings to see its stock rise immediately after a report</cite>.

takes.event_basis: Q1 FY27 Earnings on 2026-05-22 (after market close). CEO Huang's participation in US-China summit signals potential export pathway recovery; hyperscaler capex spending on track for $725B+ combined annually.
takes.thesis: <cite index="31-8">CEO Huang's participation in US delegation summit with China suggests potential pathway for advanced chip exports, removing regulatory headwinds on Chinese demand.</cite> Combined with sustained AI capex momentum and <cite index="6-9">hyperscaler combined 2026 spending projections tracking near $725 billion, above earlier expectations,</cite> NVIDIA positioned for earnings beat and raised guidance.
takes.risks: Geopolitical detente could reverse if US-Iran tensions escalate; inflation surprise may prompt Warsh Fed to signal rate-hold policy extending into H2 2026.

takes.event_basis: Intel diversification deal validation; path to WWDC (June 4) with expected AI announcements. <cite index="47-4">Apple hit all-time intraday high of $290.33</cite>, now at $300.
takes.thesis: <cite index="32-5">Deal would gain steady stream of demand for Intel foundry, key milestone for contract manufacturing</cite>. WWDC catalyst sets up product strategy for 2026-27 iPhone/Mac cycle with manufacturing optionality. Supply chain diversification reduces TSMC concentration risk.
takes.risks: Stock at all-time highs with sentiment elevated; any product announcement shortfall or delayed Intel ramp could trigger profit-taking.

takes.event_basis: <cite index="45-4,45-8">WWDC26 will spotlight incredible updates for Apple platforms, including AI advancements and exciting new software and developer tools</cite>; <cite index="45-14">WWDC kicks off with the Keynote and Platforms State of the Union on Monday, June 8</cite>.
takes.thesis: <cite index="44-19">iOS 27 is expected to introduce a much more capable Siri, with Apple reportedly preparing a long-delayed Siri 2.0 experience</cite>. <cite index="50-5">WWDC 2026 could finally get the Gemini-powered Siri that's so eagerly anticipated</cite>. <cite index="6-3">The company was reported to have reached a preliminary agreement for Intel to manufacture some of Apple's chips -- a notable vote of confidence given Apple's long-running reliance on Taiwan Semiconductor</cite>, validating Apple's supply chain diversification thesis.
takes.risks: AI announcements at WWDC may be incremental; Siri upgrade execution risk remains; no major iPhone/hardware updates expected until fall 2026, limiting stock catalysts post-WWDC.

takes.event_basis: <cite index="27-1">Prices rose 0.6% on a monthly basis, driving the annual rate to 3.8%, the highest since May 2023</cite>; foundry customer validation (Apple, Nvidia) expected through H2 2026; <cite index="6-23">Intel stock notched a fresh 52-week high near $130 after Q1 2026 earnings and the preliminary Apple agreement</cite>.
takes.thesis: <cite index="6-30">Intel's first-quarter 2026 revenue rose 7% year over year to $13.6 billion, beating the midpoint of management's own guidance by more than $1 billion and marking the sixth consecutive quarter Intel has exceeded its own forecast</cite>. <cite index="6-11">The Apple, Nvidia, and Terafab deals all suggest the foundry strategy is gaining real traction, and the data center and AI business is finally showing some momentum</cite>.
takes.risks: <cite index="6-12,6-13">The stock has already priced a lot of that in; valuation metrics suggest a pullback or execution milestones required before further re-rating</cite>. Q1 earnings beat expectations, but 18A-P process validation with actual customer shipments still pending.
